attributes.description: Alerts when peak write I/O latency exceeds a threshold. Uses the Maximum statistic for worst-case tail latency. Correlate with DiskQueueDepth and WriteIOPS for storage bottlenecks.
attributes.name: [AWS RDS OTel] Write latency high

investigation_guide.blob:
## Write Latency High

### Triage
1. Check `DiskQueueDepth` and whether `WriteIOPS` is pinned at the provisioned ceiling.
2. Inspect `BurstBalance` on gp2 volumes for credit depletion.
3. Review write-heavy workloads — bulk inserts, large transactions, or checkpoint pressure.
4. Compare `WriteThroughput` trends to storage provisioned capacity.

### Mitigation
- Increase provisioned IOPS or migrate to gp3/io2.
- Batch writes or reduce transaction size.
- Scale storage or instance if compute is the bottleneck.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/RDS"
    AND MetricName == "WriteLatency"
    AND stat == "Maximum"
| STATS max_write_latency = MAX(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/WriteLatency`)
    BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
// Seconds per I/O — adjust sensitivity (default: 50 ms)
| WHERE max_write_latency > 0.05
| SORT max_write_latency DESC
